page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 22 vCPUs and 88 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 11 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, translating to 2.0 threads per core, and utilizes dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 105 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks place the c3-standard-22 in the average tier across most standardized tests. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1715.0 and a multi-core score of 12159.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 28524.38. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1498.41 GB/sec, which drops to 107.14 GB/sec for large block uncached reads, and a write bandwidth of 46.01 GB/sec. The system demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, notably achieving 312.98 tokens per second in text generation for a small 135M parameter LLM, placing it in the top 25% tier.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated matrix operations. These hardware features support workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and Redis key-value stores, where it handles 24.14 million SET operations per second. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and moderate machine learning inference tasks that leverage CPU-based matrix acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 22 vCPUs and 88 GB of RAM, utilizing an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance features 11 physical cores with dedicated CPU allocation, a 105 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). Benchmark results show average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a PassMark CPU score of 28524.38 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12159.0. Notably, it demonstrates strong text generation performance on small language models, reaching 312.98 tokens per second. The c3-standard-22 is suited for balanced workloads including software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 22 dedicated vCPUs, 88 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions for balanced general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22 features 22 vCPUs, 88 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, delivering balanced performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors with advanced matrix extensions for general-purpose and CPU-accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: 22 dedicated vCPUs and 88 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor.
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction sets.
Delivers 312.98 tokens/sec text generation for small LLM inference.
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators included.
Well-suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
